message:
refactor(lexical/cass-compat): rewrite edge-ngram generation around char-boundary index slice

Reshape `cass_generate_edge_ngrams` so prefix generation operates over a
pre-collected slice of char-boundary indices rather than a running
`char_count` + `start_idx + ch.len_utf8()` calculation inside the loop.

Behavioural notes:
- The cap is now expressed as `MAX_NGRAM_INDICES = 21` (one char-boundary
  index plus the word end marker for a 20-char prefix), which preserves the
  previous 20-char maximum.
- Words shorter than 2 chars are still skipped (old: `char_count < 2`
  continue; new: `indices.len() < 3` continue — indices includes the
  end-of-word sentinel).
- Prefix strings produced are identical to the previous implementation; only
  the iteration strategy changes.

Also add `cass_build_preview_preserves_existing_behavior` regression test
to pin down the empty/short/UTF-8 ellipsis truncation paths for
`cass_build_preview`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
